description: Modal 'po' presents an event as something that can or may occur. Context decides whether the opening comes from ability, permission, or circumstances. The modal 'na' (NEC) asserts necessity instead.
articulatory_notes: Closed lips release 'p' into rounded true-mid 'o'. The entire particle fits in one stressed syllable.
usage_notes: The modal rank lies between evidentiality and negation. 'To po' moves possibility into the past, 'so po' into the future, and 'po ma' denies possibility: cannot. Only one modal particle belongs in a clause.
